# Questions in Livestream 200

Live Stream #200: The Terrible Two Hundreds!!!

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/wQkBymcVTEs?t=812" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:32</a> | Why are there only two cherubim on the ark when throne visions show four creatures? | He said the number changes with the geometry of the sacred space being guarded. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/wQkBymcVTEs?t=1881" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:21</a> | Was Isaac old enough to fight back against Abraham? | He guessed Isaac was around 13 and said the text does not require a dramatic physical struggle to make the scene work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/wQkBymcVTEs?t=1997" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:17</a> | Why does it seem like God never helps when I ask for help? | He framed that as part of God's hiddenness and said people often miss help that is present or do not yet see the good in delayed help. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/wQkBymcVTEs?t=2367" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:27</a> | Why was God angry at Balaam if Balaam went after God's second command in Numbers 22? | He said the permission was qualified and Balaam's inner intent remained crooked, which is why the trip still drew opposition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/wQkBymcVTEs?t=3028" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:28</a> | Could you kill a god by destroying its idol? | No. He said the image could be damaged or symbolically mutilated, but that was not the same as killing the deity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/wQkBymcVTEs?t=5424" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:24</a> | How should the Hebrew word hokmah be translated? | He gave "wisdom" as the basic translation, with a practical range that can include skill, craft, knowledge, or cleverness depending on context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/wQkBymcVTEs?t=5542" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:22</a> | How should someone respond to a person strongly attached to Ron Wyatt claims? | He warned that direct confrontation can trigger defensive conspiracy thinking and recommended asking questions that test the claims against Scripture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/wQkBymcVTEs?t=8157" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:57</a> | How was polygamy or bigamy viewed in ancient Egyptian society? | He said it was generally not socially accepted for ordinary Egyptians, though kings were an exception and divorce or remarriage was relatively easy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/wQkBymcVTEs?t=8289" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:09</a> | Did Egyptians believe their gods died forever and disappeared? | He said no. Egyptian gods could become weak, ineffective, or even die and enter the afterlife, but they were not understood simply to vanish forever. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/wQkBymcVTEs?t=8351" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:11</a> | Since Christ means anointed, do ancient kings take that title for themselves? | He said generally no. "Anointed" is a religious designation others may apply, but ancient kings did not normally take "Messiah" as a self-chosen royal title. |
